expectations.text: Response uses nuqs parseAs* functions for typed URL params
expectations.evidence: productSearchParams uses parseAsString, parseAsInteger, parseAsBoolean, parseAsStringLiteral for q, category, min, max, in_stock, sort, page parameters

expectations.text: Response implements useQueryStates for multiple params
expectations.evidence: FilterPanel and Toolbar both use: const [params, setParams] = useQueryStates(productSearchParams) to manage all params together

expectations.text: Response resets page to 1 when filters change
expectations.evidence: updateFilter function includes: setParams({ ...updates, page: 1 }); // Always reset page. Toolbar sort change also sets page: 1

expectations.text: Response integrates with TanStack Router validateSearch
expectations.evidence: Route uses: validateSearch: createStandardSchemaV1(productSearchParams, { partialOutput: true }) to bridge nuqs params into TanStack Router's search validation

expectations.text: Response uses clearOnDefault for clean URLs
expectations.evidence: Response explains clearOnDefault behavior: 'page=1, in_stock=false, sort=newest, and q= (empty) are all absent from the URL when they equal their defaults -- this is clearOnDefault in action, keeping URLs clean and shareable'. Parsers use .withDefault() which enables clearOnDefault by default in nuqs
